author: terencechain
content: but block hash commits to the parent hash right? i dont follow how could its grand parent block hash diverge here

author: potuz
content: Not the parent but the grandparent

author: potuz
content: Since the CL hasn't verified the parent's block asserted parent_block_hash. It's a trivial fix I think

author: m.kalinin
content:
> So if I understand this correctly, when I send the first gossipped payload to the engine, and the engine tells me VALID, I am guaranteed that its parent block had the right blockhash even though I never checked it. But I do not get that guarantee about it's grandparent.

The parent has the right block hash which is written in the parent's beacon block, but suppose the parent's beacon block is malicious and has `feeRecipient: potuz.eth` while the EL block that EL has validated has `feeRecipient: mkalinin.eth` -- you cannot verify this unless you have run a `block_hash` check on the parent, i.e. `hash(rlp(parent_beacon_block.execution_payload)) == parent_beacon_block.execution_payload.block_hash`

author: m.kalinin
content: I am not 100% sure but it seems to me that for Gloas the full `block_hash` validation can be replaced with verifying that `bid` field values matches the corresponding values in the payload with the `bid.block_hash` for each beacon block that CL has imported

author: m.kalinin
content: the `fee_recipient` example doesn't work 😄 then let's instead of `fee_recipient` say that the first withdrawal that beacon chain enforces has `address: potuz.eth` while the payload has this withdrawal with `address: mkalinin.eth`, then the same issue applies to this case
